items.summary: EX-West Ham ace Michail Antonio is to face trial after having a driving ­conviction quashed. items.summary: MANCHESTER CITY are closing in on Elliot Anderson - with Patrick Vieira convinced they will be getting a 'complete midfielder'. items.summary: NEVER mind Wonderwall, England should bring back All Together Now as their tournament theme song. items.title: Ecclestone backs Dean's return as captain after Sciver-Brunt injury
items.summary: Sophie Ecclestone expects Charlie Dean to return to the England captaincy "like a duck to water" for their next two matches at the Women's T20 World Cup.

items.title: Oval Test becoming 'Root's worst nightmare' as interim captain
items.summary: Stuart Broad says the second Test is becoming England interim captain Joe Root's "worst nightmare" with New Zealand in a dominant position against an inexperienced home side.

items.summary: GRANIT XHAKA is no stranger to controversy, which is probably why he always seems to shake it off with ease. items.summary: ENGLAND can seal top place in World Cup Group L with a match to spare on Tuesday - after Fifa changed the World Cup rules. items.summary: DALLAS has been smashed with life-threatening floods just days before Lionel Messi and world champions Argentina pay a visit. items.title: Janse van Rensburg makes debut as England fall to France in non-cap international
items.summary: Benhard Janse van Rensburg’s hopes of a try-scoring England debut were dashed by fumbling hands as France emerged conclusive 35-19 winners in their non-cap international in Vannes.

items.summary: BARROW AFC have had a pop at Fifa after one of their fans was denied entry to an England game because of the club's badge. items.title: Portugal's Dias: Ronaldo criticism 'nothing new'
items.summary: Portugal defender Rúben Dias on Friday branded criticism of teammate Cristiano Ronaldo "insignificant" and claimed it has not affected the team's focus.

items.title: Arsenal start new PL season against Coventry
items.summary: Arsenal will begin their Premier League title defence against newly-promoted Coventry City, while Liverpool travel to face Newcastle in the pick of the opening-day fixtures.

items.title: Pulisic (calf) out of USMNT's clash with Australia...
items.summary: United States star Christian Pulisic is out of the team's World Cup clash with Australia with a calf injury, with Ricardo Pepi starting in his place.

items.title: Korea GK gaffe helps Mexico clinch 1st in Group A
items.summary: Mexico took advantage of a defensive blunder by South Korea to win 1-0 on Thursday and become the first team to advance to the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Canada's Koné stretchered off after hard tackle
items.summary: Canada midfielder Ismaël Koné was stretchered off the field in the second half of the host nation's match against Qatar on Thursday at BC Place in Vancouver.

items.title: Aguirre relishes Azteca return after Group A win
items.summary: Mexico beat South Korea 1-0 to become the first team to advance to the World Cup knockout stage, securing the top spot in Group A and a dream Round of 32 match at the Estadio Azteca.
